hypothesis: Under DAC-relevant conditions (298 K, 0.4 mbar CO2, 40% RH), branched polyamine sorbents engineered to contain 25–35 mol% tertiary amines randomly distributed within a predominantly secondary-amine network (total amine density 8–10 mmol g−1) will achieve at least 15% higher equilibrium CO2 uptake than otherwise identical sorbents lacking tertiary amines.
